entry.title: Getting Started with .NET 8 - A Beginner's Guide
entry.summary: .NET 8 is here, and it's packed with exciting features that make it the perfect time to start your .NET journey! Whether you're a complete beginner or coming from another programming language, this guide will help you take your first steps.
entry.content:
.NET 8 is here, and it's packed with exciting features that make it the perfect time to start your .NET journey! Whether you're a complete beginner or coming from another programming language, this guide will help you take your first steps.

Why .NET 8?​

.NET 8 brings several improvements that make it ideal for beginners:

  • Better performance - Faster startup times and improved memory usage
  • Simplified development - Less code needed for common tasks
  • Cross-platform - Write once, run on Windows, macOS, and Linux
  • Great tooling - Excellent IDE support and debugging capabilities
  • Huge ecosystem - Thousands of packages available through NuGet

What You'll Need​

Before we start coding, let's set up your development environment:

1. Install .NET 8 SDK​

Visit dot.net and download the latest .NET 8 SDK for your operating system.

2. Choose Your IDE​

  • Visual Studio Community (Windows/Mac) - Full-featured IDE, perfect for beginners
  • Visual Studio Code (All platforms) - Lightweight, great for learning
  • JetBrains Rider - Premium option with advanced features

3. Verify Installation​

Open your terminal and run:

dotnet --version

You should see something like 8.0.100 or higher.

Your First .NET 8 Application​

Let's create a simple console application:

dotnet new console -n HelloKenya
cd HelloKenya
dotnet run

This creates and runs a basic "Hello, World!" application.

Making It Kenyan​

Let's customize our application:

Console.WriteLine("Habari! Welcome to .NET 8! 🇰🇪");
Console.WriteLine($"Today is {DateTime.Now:dddd, MMMM dd, yyyy}");

Console.Write("What's your name? ");
string? name = Console.ReadLine();

if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(name))
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Karibu {name}! Welcome to the .NET community!");
    Console.WriteLine("Ready to build amazing applications? Let's go! 🚀");
}

What Makes .NET 8 Special?​

Minimal APIs​

Building web APIs has never been easier:

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
var app = builder.Build();

app.MapGet("/", () => "Habari from Kenya! 🇰🇪");
app.MapGet("/time", () => DateTime.Now.ToString("F"));

app.Run();

This creates a web API with just a few lines of code!

Top-Level Statements​

Notice how we don't need a Main method or class declarations? .NET 8 uses top-level statements to reduce boilerplate code.

New Performance Features​

  • Native AOT - Compile to native code for faster startup
  • Source generators - Better compile-time code generation
  • Improved garbage collection - Better memory management

Learning Path for Kenyan Developers​

Based on our local job market, here's what we recommend learning:

  1. C# Fundamentals - Variables, methods, classes
  2. ASP.NET Core - Web development (high demand in Kenya)
  3. Entity Framework Core - Database interactions
  4. Blazor - Build web UIs with C#
  5. Azure - Cloud deployment and services

Local Context Examples​

Throughout your learning, try building projects relevant to our local context:

  • M-Pesa Integration - Payment processing
  • County Government System - Public service applications
  • Agricultural Management - Farm management systems
  • Education Platform - E-learning for local schools
  • Healthcare System - Patient management systems

Resources for Learning​

Online Courses​

  • Microsoft Learn - Free official courses
  • Our own .NET Kenya Courses - Localized content

Books​

  • "C# 12 and .NET 8 – Modern Cross-Platform Development" by Mark J. Price
  • "ASP.NET Core in Action" by Andrew Lock

Practice Platforms​

  • HackerRank - Coding challenges
  • LeetCode - Algorithm practice
  • GitHub - Build your portfolio

Join Our Community​

Learning is better together! Join our community:

  • Monthly Meetups - In-person and virtual sessions
  • Study Groups - Practice with fellow beginners
  • Mentorship Program - Get paired with experienced developers
  • Job Board - Access to .NET opportunities in Kenya

Next Steps​

Ready to dive deeper? Here's what to do next:

  1. Complete our .NET Fundamentals course
  2. Join our next meetup
  3. Follow us on social media for daily tips and updates
  4. Start building - Pick a small project and begin coding!

Common Beginner Questions​

Q: Is .NET only for Windows? A: No! .NET runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Q: Do I need to pay for Visual Studio? A: Visual Studio Community is free for individuals and small teams.

Q: How long does it take to learn .NET? A: With consistent practice, you can build basic applications in 2-3 months.

Q: Are there job opportunities in Kenya? A: Yes! Many companies in Kenya use .NET for enterprise applications.

Ready to start your .NET 8 journey? We're here to support you every step of the way!

Furahia kuprogramu! (Enjoy programming!)

entry.title: Welcome to .NET Kenya Community!
entry.summary: We're thrilled to launch the official website for the .NET Kenya Community! This marks a significant milestone in our journey to build a vibrant and supportive ecosystem for .NET developers across Kenya and East Africa.
entry.content:
We're thrilled to launch the official website for the .NET Kenya Community! This marks a significant milestone in our journey to build a vibrant and supportive ecosystem for .NET developers across Kenya and East Africa.

Our Mission​

The .NET Kenya Community exists to:

  • Connect developers passionate about .NET technologies
  • Share knowledge through meetups, workshops, and online resources
  • Support career growth and professional development
  • Collaborate on open source projects and community initiatives
  • Celebrate the achievements of our members

What You'll Find Here​

Our new website serves as your central hub for everything .NET Kenya:

🗓️ Events​

Stay updated on our regular meetups, workshops, and special events. Each event page includes speaker information, presentation materials, and recordings when available.

📚 Courses​

Access our growing library of courses designed specifically for Kenyan developers. From .NET fundamentals to advanced topics, all with local context and real-world examples.

✍️ Blog​

Read community news, technical articles, career advice, and insights from fellow developers in the Kenyan tech ecosystem.

📞 Contact​

Learn how to get involved, become a speaker, volunteer, or just connect with the community.

Join Our Growing Community​

Whether you're a seasoned .NET architect or just starting your programming journey, there's a place for you in our community. We believe in:

  • Inclusive learning - Everyone starts somewhere, and we're here to support each other
  • Knowledge sharing - Your experiences, however small, can help others grow
  • Professional growth - Connect with mentors, find job opportunities, and advance your career
  • Fun and friendship - Technology is better when shared with great people!

Get Connected​

  • Twitter: @dotnetke
  • Meetup: .NET Kenya Community
  • GitHub: github.com/dotnetke
  • LinkedIn: .NET Kenya Community

What's Next?​

We have exciting plans for 2024:

  • Monthly technical meetups
  • Hands-on workshops and bootcamps
  • Guest speakers from international tech companies
  • Open source project collaborations
  • Career development sessions
  • Social events and networking opportunities

Thank You​

A huge thanks to all the volunteers who made this website possible and continue to drive our community forward. Special recognition goes to our founding members who believed in this vision from day one.

Ready to start your journey with us? Check out our upcoming events or dive into our courses!

Welcome to the family! 🚀

Karibu sana! (You're very welcome!)
